source: ICH S6(R1); G-CSF prescribing information; ScienceDirect Topics (splenic rupture case reports)
description: Expected findings for granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (filgrastim, pegfilgrastim). Primary pharmacology is neutrophil production and release from marrow. Rat, monkey, and dog are relevant species. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Extreme leukocytosis (WBC >100 x 10^9/L) or leukostasis = adverse. Splenic rupture = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Primary pharmacodynamic effect of G-CSF; stimulus for neutrophil production and release from marrow. (G-CSF prescribing information)
Reflects neutrophil-driven increase in total WBC.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Near-total granulocytic replacement of marrow = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Expected histological correlate of G-CSF pharmacology.
G-CSF mobilizes stem cells to spleen; granulocytic extramedullary hematopoiesis. Spontaneous splenic rupture documented as rare adverse event. (ScienceDirect Topics)
Granulocytic extramedullary hematopoiesis drives spleen weight increase.
